speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been feeling really unwell since yesterday morning.

speaker: doctor
text: Hello — thanks for coming in. Can you tell me more about what’s been going on?

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature just came back at 39.8 °C — that’s quite high. When did the fever start?

speaker: patient
text: It started last night — and I’ve been extremely tired all day, like no energy at all.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin is 138 g/L — that’s within normal range, so anemia isn’t likely contributing to the fatigue.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also had some dizziness when standing up quickly, and my blood pressure has been running high lately.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your history and today’s readings, I’m diagnosing essential hypertension — we’ll need to monitor this closely.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been taking my medications regularly, but I’m not sure if they’re working as well as before.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re currently on Metformin 1000mg twice daily — that’s correct for your type 2 diabetes management.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose today is 7.7 mmol/L — slightly above target, but not alarming yet.

speaker: patient
text: I checked it again before coming in — it was 7.6, so I thought it was stable.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s also review your cardiovascular risk factors — do you have any chest discomfort or shortness of breath?

speaker: patient
text: No chest pain, but I’ve had a dry cough for two days and my head feels heavy.

speaker: doctor
text: Your repeat glucose reading just now is 9.9 mmol/L — that’s notably higher than the earlier value. Were you eating or stressed before this test?

speaker: patient
text: I had a small snack an hour ago — maybe that affected it?

speaker: doctor
text: That could explain part of it. Let’s also check if infection is playing a role — your fever suggests possible systemic involvement.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been drinking lots of water and resting, but the fever hasn’t broken completely.

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is now down to 38.6 °C — still elevated, but trending downward. We’ll keep monitoring.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your elevated cardiovascular risk and current lipid profile, I’m going to start you on Atorvastatin 20mg once daily at bedtime.

speaker: patient
text: Okay — and just to confirm, that reading of 7.7 mmol/L from earlier is still my baseline, right?
